repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §1.2 Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis covers switch-on coefficient decompositions under exact no-anticipation-style history conventions; doc/API.md §3 says PartialID catalogue is empty.

published_prior_art.cite: AugustinGutknechtLiu2025 (https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.20415) studies bias under anticipation/misclassification and modified estimators/tests, but not this covariate-conditional box support decomposition.

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends: aggregate pretrend-summary sufficiency/nonsharpness, not bounded anticipation boxes for treated and comparison cohorts; PartialID catalogue empty in doc/API.md §3.

published_prior_art.cite: RambachanRoth2023 (https://colab.ws/articles/10.1093/restud/rdad018) supplies general partial-ID sensitivity logic for parallel-trends violations, but not a staggered-adoption anticipation-bias operator with shared-coordinate aggregate pooling.

repo_prior_art.cite: Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis: no-anticipation point-formula analogue for switch-on coefficients, not the bounded-anticipation ATT interval collapse.

published_prior_art.cite: CallawaySantAnna2021 (https://ideas.repec.org/a/eee/econom/v225y2021i2p200-230.html) and DCDH2020 (https://www.aeaweb.org/articles?id=10.1257/aer.20181169) are the stated zero-bound point-ID benchmarks.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Promote the variation-independent anticipation-box condition from a scope assumption into an explicit compatibility lemma or example family, so sharpness is not perceived as assumed by definition.
Add a worked three-cohort example where a not-yet-treated comparison cohort anticipates at t but not b, showing coefficient pooling before absolute values for an aggregate.
Tighten the DCDH zero-bound clause by defining exactly which switcher/non-switcher weights satisfy the proposal's normalization convention.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 as the flagship and Theorems 1-2 treated as algebraic/reduction scaffolding.
